component.name: Link
component.description: Takes people to a new location in the app or on another site. Renders a real anchor; for offsite destinations it shows an external icon and opens a new tab.
component.note: Inter 14/20. default = blue.500 underlined; subtle = neutral, underline on hover; inverse = white for dark surfaces. Hover/press are CSS pseudo-states. Synced to Figma 'Link'.

tokens.gap: spacing.2xs (~4px) between text and external icon

appearances.default: colors.text.link.default (blue.500); hover → blue.700; press → blue.800; always underlined
appearances.subtle: colors.text.neutral.weak (neutral.500); no underline by default, underline on hover; press → colors.text.neutral.strong (neutral.800)
appearances.inverse: colors.text.neutral.inverse (white); hover/press → blue.100; for dark surfaces

tokens.externalIcon: Icon 'external-link' at 14px, inherits link color, not underlined
tokens.focusRing: 2px colors.border.primary.strong (forest.700); inverse uses white for contrast

useCases: Navigate within the app or to another page
Open an external resource (docs, policy, third-party site)
A low-emphasis navigation affordance where a button would be too heavy

commonPatterns.name: Inline text link
commonPatterns.description: A default link inside a paragraph; underlined so it is distinguishable from surrounding text.
commonPatterns.jsx: Read the <Link href='/report'>full report</Link> for details.

commonPatterns.name: External link
commonPatterns.description: Offsite destination — external icon + opens a new tab with safe rel.

commonPatterns.name: Subtle link
commonPatterns.description: Lower-emphasis navigation (e.g. a 'view all' affordance) where blue would be too loud.

commonPatterns.name: Inverse on dark
commonPatterns.description: Links over a dark banner or footer.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a Link to trigger an action (submit, delete, toggle)
antiPatterns.reason: Links navigate; actions that mutate state should look and behave like buttons.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Button (text appearance for low emphasis).

antiPatterns.scenario: A link with no href (or href='#') used as a button
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Button, or give the Link a real destination.

antiPatterns.scenario: Removing the underline from default links inside body text
antiPatterns.reason: Color alone is not a reliable link affordance within paragraphs.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep default (underlined); reserve subtle for standalone links.

antiPatterns.scenario: Opening internal navigation in a new tab
antiPatterns.reason: Unexpected new tabs disorient users; reserve new tabs for genuinely external destinations.
antiPatterns.alternative: Only set isExternal for offsite links.

slots.externalIcon: Auto-rendered after the text when isExternal

interactions.subtle: neutral.500; underline appears on hover; press → neutral.800

behavior.external: Sets target=_blank and rel='noopener noreferrer' (both overridable); announces 'opens in a new tab' via visually-hidden text.

accessibility.focusRing: 2px forest.700 outline, 2px offset; inverse uses white
accessibility.screenReader: External links append a visually-hidden '(opens in a new tab)'; the icon is aria-hidden
accessibility.contrastNote: blue.500 (#2f68ff) on white is 4.6:1 (AA for text). Default links keep an underline so they are distinguishable by more than color.

athenaConstraints.linkColor: blue.500 (the one place blue leads, distinct from the forest brand action color)

figmaDivergences.reason: Matches the rest of the system; browser-driven.

aiHints.context: Reach for Link for navigation — within the app or offsite. Use default (underlined) inside body text, subtle for low-emphasis standalone links, inverse on dark surfaces, and isExternal for offsite destinations. For actions that mutate state use a Button (text appearance for low emphasis).
neverUseWhen: The control triggers an action rather than navigation — use Button
There is no real href — use Button
It is inside body text and you want to drop the underline — keep default underlined
The destination is internal but you want a new tab — don't set isExternal
